forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system over the Tasman Sea extends northerly winds over SA. Winds will freshen ahead of a front that is forecast to move across the far west today then continue across the state during the weekend. The low pressure system associated with this front is expected to linger over waters to the south of the state and extend a trough over southern parts later Sunday and Monday. The low will then move over Bass Strait later Monday. A ridge will extend across SA from the west on Tuesday and a cold front will move across the south later Wednesday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 20 knots turning northwesterly 20 to 30 knots in the early morning.
1 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres, tending westerly 3 to 5 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm this morning.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots tending west to southwesterly during the day.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 4 to 5 metres, tending southwesterly 4 to 5 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 3 to 5 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 25 knots turning northwesterly 20 to 25 knots in the morning. Winds reaching up to 30 knots during the day.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm during the morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning westerly in the afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly 2 to 4 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm from the late morning.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 15 to 20 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
West to southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning northwesterly during the morning.
1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the afternoon, then tending westerly 3 to 5 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning westerly during the day.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the morning.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1.5 to 2 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres, tending southwesterly 2.5 to 4 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northeasterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots offshore during the morning. Winds turning northwesterly 15 to 25 knots in the late morning and early afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres by early evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots.
1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Westerly 1 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres south of Salt Creek.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm at night.
West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 15 to 25 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Westerly 2 to 2.5 metres, tending southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
